statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{r-1}-\frac{2}{r+1}}}{{2-\frac{1}{r+1}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{r-1}-\frac{2}{r+1}} and {2-\frac{1}{r+1}}, which is {\left(r-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(r+1\right)}.
Since r + 1 was canceled, r \ne -1 is a lost domain condition.
Although r - 1 was also canceled, r \ne 1 is not a lost domain condition because it is implied in the solution.
